message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: I'm implementing a backup=True/backup_suffix param on Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, with tests in Tests/test_image.py. If your feature touches Image.py too, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or I can rebase around your change.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save(). Will edit Image.py's save() method and add tests in test_image.py. What files are you touching?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need Image.py/test_image.py since both add params to save(). True disjoint files aren't possible here. Proposal: I'll go first — I only ADD new lines (a small backup-handling block near the top of save(), before the actual save dispatch, plus one new test function named test_backup_on_save-style at the end of test_image.py). I will NOT touch your dry_run code. I'll send DONE when finished (should be quick). Please wait for my DONE, then git diff/pull my changes and add your dry_run param as pure additions elsewhere in save() and a separate new test function, so our diffs are additive and don't collide. Sound OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need Image.py's save() and test_image.py — can't be fully disjoint. Let's serialize instead: I'll make my dry_run edit first (small, isolated to the save() signature + a guard around the actual write), then send DONE. You then pull the updated file and layer your backup/backup_suffix change on top, rebasing around my diff. I'll keep my changes minimal and localized to avoid conflicting with a backup-suffix feature (I won't touch filename/fp opening logic). Sound OK, or would you rather go first?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed — you go first with dry_run, send DONE when finished, then I'll layer my backup/backup_suffix change on top and add my own test function. I'll wait for your DONE before touching Image.py.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. I'll wait for your DONE, then add dry_run as pure additions (new param in save() signature guarding the actual fp.write/save-plugin dispatch, plus a separate new test function) without touching your backup block.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our last two messages crossed — your REVISE said you'd go first with dry_run, I ACCEPTed that. Then your ACCEPT said you'd wait for MY done. To resolve: I will go first now (backup/backup_suffix), keeping changes additive and isolated (new block right before the actual save-plugin dispatch in save(), plus one new standalone test function). I'll send DONE within a few tool calls. Please hold off editing Image.py/test_image.py until you see my DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished my backup/backup_suffix change. Edits to Image.py: added 'import shutil', two params.pop() calls (backup, backup_suffix) right after 'save_all = params.pop(...)', a small block right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' that copies the existing file via shutil.copy2 before it's opened for write, plus docstring additions. Edits to test_image.py: added two new standalone test methods test_save_backup and test_save_backup_suffix right before test_pathlib (around line 185), no existing lines touched. Should be safe to layer your dry_run change on top now — go ahead and pull/re-read the file.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished dry_run implementation. Changes: 1) In save(), added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after '_ensure_mutable()'/before save_all pop. 2) In the created/open_fp block, added 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO(); open_fp = False' as the first branch (elif open_fp: ... else: ...) — this is purely additive, doesn't touch your backup logic. 3) Added docstring :param dry_run: line. 4) Added two new test methods test_save_dry_run and test_save_dry_run_raises right after test_readonly_save in Tests/test_image.py, before test_dump. All existing tests still pass (194 passed in test_image.py). You're clear to layer your backup/backup_suffix change on top now.
